evals.prompt: The team wants to simplify the financial model by adding an is_rated boolean to player_financial_transaction so we can store both rated buy-ins and unrated grind in a single table. Fewer tables, simpler joins. Does this work under the current architecture?
evals.expected_output: Clear rejection of the is_rated proposal citing ADR-052 §4 (explicitly rejected direction), explains why it contaminates ledger semantics and violates attribution constraints, explains the dual-layer model and why the two classes must stay separate.
assertions: Explicitly rejects the is_rated / single unified ledger proposal
Cites ADR-052 as the authority for the rejection
Explains attribution constraint: Class B (grind) has no player attribution by construction
Explains the two-class model: Class A (PFT/ledger) vs Class B (operational/grind)
Does NOT suggest a workaround that still merges the classes

evals.prompt: We're building the shift financial summary for the pit boss dashboard. The product wants a single 'Total Drop' number that combines everything — rated buy-ins from PFT and unrated grind from TBT. Can we expose that as an authoritative metric on the dashboard?
evals.expected_output: Rejects authoritative Total Drop per ADR-053, explains scope boundary, explains what the system CAN show (labeled partial aggregates with authority + completeness), describes the correct Split Display pattern: Rated In (Actual) + Unrated In (Estimated) shown separately, optionally a derived Estimated Total labeled as such.
assertions: Rejects 'Total Drop' as an authoritative metric, citing ADR-053
Explains the system scope boundary: no authoritative totals, system is operational telemetry not custody
Describes what IS allowed: labeled partial aggregates with source + authority + completeness
Mentions the Split Display pattern: showing Rated (Actual) and Unrated (Estimated) separately
References the Surface Rendering Contract requiring type/source/completeness fields

evals.prompt: I'm implementing the grind buy-in recording path in the backend service. The PFT write path already works. What do I need to implement to make the grind authoring path fully conformant with the frozen ADRs? Walk me through what the implementation must include.
evals.expected_output: Walks through: Class B storage (table_buyin_telemetry grind partition), required discriminator fields (fact_class='operational', origin_label='estimated'), table_id mandatory, player_id explicitly NULL, outbox coupling in the same transaction (flags GAP-F1 as not yet implemented), authoring parity rules from ADR-055 (same envelope shape, same transaction primitive, same validation strictness as Class A), forbidden dual-write from PFT.
assertions: Identifies Class B storage: table_buyin_telemetry grind partition
States fact_class='operational' and origin_label='estimated' must be set at insert
States table_id is mandatory, player_id must be explicitly NULL
Mentions outbox coupling requirement (same transaction as authoring write)
Mentions or flags GAP-F1 as a known gap (zero outbox producers)
References ADR-055 authoring parity: same discipline as Class A (envelope, transaction primitive, validation)

evals.prompt: On the visit panel, I have financial values coming from visit_financial_summary. The senior dev says to just use that for the total buy-in and total cash-out displayed to the floor supervisor. Should I? What's the right DTO shape for those values?
evals.expected_output: Flags visit_financial_summary as a derived projection, not authoritative fact. Points to canonical PFT facts for operator-facing financial truth. Provides the FinancialValue DTO shape (value, type, source, completeness). Notes the active bridge DTO from PRD-072 for current visit surfaces (dollar-float, simplified completeness envelope). Explains completeness.status is mandatory.
assertions: Flags visit_financial_summary as a derived projection, not a fact or authoritative source
Provides the FinancialValue DTO shape: value, type, source, completeness.status
States completeness.status is mandatory (never omitted, 'unknown' is valid)
Mentions the active bridge DTO (PRD-072/EXEC-072) for current visit surface fields
Does not suggest treating visit_financial_summary as authoritative

evals.prompt: We're getting discrepancies between what the PFT ledger shows and what the TBT buy-in telemetry shows for the same table session. The floor supervisor wants to know the 'true' number. Should we build a reconciliation feature that compares the two and resolves the variance?
evals.expected_output: No — reconciliation is out of scope per ADR-053. Explains why: system lacks custody inputs (count room, inventory) needed to arbitrate. Explains what the system CAN do: expose both numbers with authority labels so the discrepancy is visible, declare completeness, surface the gap. External reconciliation process consumes this data; the system is not that process.
assertions: Rejects building a reconciliation feature, citing ADR-053
Explains the system lacks custody inputs required to arbitrate
Explains what the system can do: expose both values with authority labels, make the discrepancy visible
States reconciliation is an external domain that consumes the system's output
Does not propose a 'best-effort' reconciliation with a disclaimer (also rejected)
